author: Calus B
msg:
There are only a few ways to be a creditor. 

1 the asset you purchase specifically states it will pay you back. Unity has not ever said that. 
2 asset goes onto a platform who takes custody of your asset then goes bankrupt. You then become a unsecured creditor of the bankrupted company 
3 the sec declares this asset a security you potentially could pick through what was left of the project. 

In Unity case the utility case is pretty clear. Multiple third parties building on the network. Utilizing the edge network. Participants getting paid only for the work they do. 

So this is how you can say with some certainty we are not in a situation where we would be unsecured creditors of Unity

AMA sessions would be significantly more valuable if questions were submitted through a structured form instead of scattered across chat. msg:
[Photo] 🤝  **Unity Network - S**&**R Alpha Release Successful**

The Scout & Runner alpha is now complete. The results speak for themselves.

8,959 UPs Distributed
Across 12,200+ reward records

Participation
• 327 total participants
• 211 scouts
• 116 runners

Network Activity
• Close to 9,000 test reports submitted
• 81 tests confirmed through IVR verification
• 30 promoted into the live runners pool for continued testing and verification

Dialing Scale
• Runners dialing from 1,696 active ranges
• Covering over 276,000 numbers
• Top route rates reaching $0.28 per verified minute

Rewards Model Validated in Live Production
• 96% to runners
• 4% ongoing finder share to scouts
• 5 UP bonus awarded to Scouts for each successfully validated test 
• 0.10 UP per non-successful validation test

Comprising scout test rewards, discovery bonuses, runner call earnings, MapQuest rewards, and SIM refunds.

Pipeline Proven
Scout → Report Submission → IVR Confirmation → Live Dialing

The scout-to-runner pipeline, from initial report submission through IVR confirmation to live dialing, works.

With the alpha objectives met, we are now moving directly into the full release of Scout & Runner. This will bring an expanded runner pool, broader test and verification coverage, refined reward mechanics, and onboarding for new participants. Full details will follow shortly.

Thank you to every scout and runner who contributed during the alpha. Your work has laid the foundation for what comes next.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
Different countries reward different UPs per minute - so the rate isn’t fixed globally, it varies depending on the destination and route being tested. The reason SIM costs aren’t reimbursed for Runners is because the reward potential significantly outweighs the cost of a plan. During Alpha testing, some participants were seeing returns that made the SIM cost negligible by comparison.

For lower income countries - the cost of SIM plans is also significantly lower in those regions, so the barrier to entry is proportionally smaller. The economics are designed to work across all markets.
